# SPEC.md — generated normative text (Hotam-Spec)

Generated from this domain's `graph.json` claims plus REAL, currently-passing `go test` runs of every `verified_by` entry, recorded via the `hotamspec` scenario API (PLAN-scenario-generated-spec.md §1/§2 D1/D2, task W1.3): the normative body under each requirement is not hand-written prose — it is the Given/When/Then/Value narrative a real test run just produced. `graph.json` remains the bookkeeping layer (id, short authored claim, status); this document is the derived projection, never the other way around. Not an enforcement gate itself — a future `check_spec_md_current` (W2.3) is the mechanical staleness floor; this generator only renders what the CURRENT run reports.

**9 requirement(s) carry `verified_by`; 2 have at least one recorded scenario narrative; 286 carry no `verified_by` yet (no code carrier, honest gap).**

## Requirements with a verified_by scenario

## `R-authored-spec-links-mechanically-checked`

**Claim:** Every requirement's implemented_by/verified_by entries (path-qualified file:symbol / file:test references into a domain's authored spec/ tree) shall be mechanically checked: the named symbol/test shall exist; a verified_by test shall be non-vacuous (a real assertion or exercising branch, not t.Log-only or empty) and free of an unconditional top-level t.Skip; the same verified_by entry shall not be formally cited by two or more requirements that are not all mutually related via Relations; and a resolvable, non-vacuous, non-skipped verified_by test shall actually PASS when compiled and executed via `go test`, not merely be shaped like a proof.

**Status:** SETTLED · **Enforcement:** ENFORCED

### `internal/invariants/authored_links_test.go:TestCheckImplementedBySymbolResolvable_MUTATION_StalenessRoundTrip`

**checkImplementedBySymbolResolvable catches a stale implemented_by symbol across a break->fix round-trip**

- Given an authored spec/ fixture whose implemented_by symbol NewRisk is present (symbol=NewRisk)
- When checkImplementedBySymbolResolvable runs against the intact fixture
- Then no staleness violation is reported (the symbol resolves) — **held**
- Given NewRisk is removed from the fixture (a rename that orphans the implemented_by reference) (removed_symbol=NewRisk)
- When checkImplementedBySymbolResolvable runs against the stale fixture
- Then a staleness violation fires for R-1 (the reference no longer resolves) — **held**
- Value (violation_count_after_mutation=1)
- Given NewRisk is restored to the fixture
- When checkImplementedBySymbolResolvable runs against the restored fixture
- Then no staleness violation is reported again (the break->fix round-trip closed) — **held**

## `R-authored-spec-projections-are-derived`

**Claim:** Every generated document that describes a domain's authored spec/ tree -- TRACEABILITY.md (requirement -> implemented_by symbol -> verified_by test), MODELS.md (an inventory of authored model structs/fields/methods/invariants extracted from spec/model via go/ast), COVERAGE.md (which SETTLED+ENFORCEABLE requirements have real implemented_by/verified_by carriers versus roadmap debt, and which layer per R-authored-spec-layer-progression the domain currently sits at), and UNENFORCED.md's authored-era burn-down -- shall be a GENERATED projection assembled by `hotam gen-spec` from the graph plus the spec/ resolver (internal/gate/spec_resolver.go), and shall NEVER be hand-written or hand-edited. spec/ itself (the domain's authored model/application/policy/tests code) is the one artifact in this pipeline authored by hand and NEVER regenerated or overwritten by the engine; docs/gen/ is the generated side and is ALWAYS safe to regenerate and overwrite.

**Status:** SETTLED · **Enforcement:** ENFORCED

## `R-no-hand-edit-graph`

**Claim:** Changes to domains/*/graph.json shall be made only through `hotam apply-proposal` / `hotam land`, with direct hand-edits prohibited outside of bootstrap events.

**Status:** SETTLED · **Enforcement:** ENFORCED

### `internal/loader/lock_real_domains_test.go:TestNoHandEditGraph_RealDomainLocksPinCurrentGraph`

**graph.json changes go only through proposal.Apply (writes graph+lock together) and a stale lock is detectable by VerifyLock**

- Given the real on-disk domains each carry a sibling graph.lock pinning their graph.json (domain_count=2)
- When VerifyLock checks each real domain's graph.lock against its graph.json
- Then every real graph.lock pins its current graph.json (no hand-edits bypassed apply-proposal/land) — **held**
- Given a minimal valid graph written to a temp dir via the sanctioned writer (no lock yet on disk)
- When proposal.Apply lands a ProposedRequirement against the temp graph (R-no-hand-edit-graph's implemented_by symbol)
- Then VerifyLock confirms the post-apply graph.lock matches the post-apply graph.json — **held**
- Then the applied proposal is actually present in the graph on disk (Apply wrote it) — **held**
- Value (applied_requirement=4 requirements after apply)

**Status:** SETTLED · **Enforcement:** ENFORCED

## `R-spec-link-embodied-vs-proven`

**Claim:** An authored-spec requirement's link to code shall carry two ORTHOGONAL fields, never collapsed into one: implemented_by names WHERE the requirement is EMBODIED (a path-qualified file:symbol reference into the domain's authored spec/ tree, the model/method that realizes the claim), and verified_by names WHERE the requirement is PROVEN (a path-qualified file:test reference, the executable test that demonstrates the claim holds). A symbol that embodies a requirement without a test that proves it, or a test cited without the symbol it exercises, is an incomplete SpecLink -- ENFORCED status requires both halves together (see R-enforced-requires-enforcer-or-authored-link), but the two fields answer two DIFFERENT questions and shall never be merged into a single field or treated as aliases of one another.

**Status:** SETTLED · **Enforcement:** ENFORCED

### `internal/proposal/proposal_test.go:TestApply_Requirement_ImplementedByAndVerifiedByAreOrthogonal`

_No scenario narrative: test passes but recorded no hotamspec scenario (plain go test, no narrative to render)._

## `R-structural-floor-vs-mirror-audit`

**Claim:** Two DIFFERENT parties hold two DIFFERENT guarantees over an authored SpecLink, and the boundary between them shall NEVER be blurred: the ENGINE holds the STRUCTURAL floor -- a cited symbol or test really exists, resolves by name against the domain's own authored spec/ tree, is not t.Log-only or otherwise empty (has teeth), is not an unconditional t.Skip, is not formally reused across unrelated requirements, and is not orphaned by a later code change -- while a MIRROR AUDIT, performed by a human or an LLM reading the pair (requirement claim text, authored code+test) without domain-specific heuristics baked into the engine, certifies that the cited test SEMANTICALLY proves the requirement's claim. The engine's structural checks shall NEVER be represented, documented, or relied upon as a substitute for the mirror audit's semantic certification, and the mirror audit's semantic judgment shall NEVER be encoded as a lexical or keyword heuristic inside the graph invariant layer.

**Status:** SETTLED · **Enforcement:** ENFORCED

| `R-decided-conflict-justifies-itself` | SETTLED | Every Conflict in DECIDED lifecycle shall carry either a non-empty rationale in DECIDED(...) or at least one derived Requirement. |

| `R-delegation-conclusions-only` | SETTLED | When an operator delegates a sub-domain, the sub-operator shall return only CONCLUSIONS with shared objects declared as an explicit border, never raw detail. |
| `R-delegation-is-a-file` | SETTLED | Every task delegation to an agent shall be recorded as a versioned file under delegations/ (DG-<n>.md, created and closed only via a dedicated delegate tool), so git carries the who/when/what history of every hand-off. |
| `R-dependency-drives-parallel` | SETTLED | Independent sub-graphs in the dependency network may be delegated to parallel sub-operators. |
| `R-dependency-drives-sequential` | SETTLED | Dependency chains in the network shall be processed sequentially. |
| `R-dependency-graph-parallelism` | REJECTED | The system shall track the dependency network between requirements/operators/entities (building on Requirement.relations depends_on/supports/refines) so that independent sub-graphs may be delegated to PARALLEL sub-operators while dependency chains are processed SEQUENTIALLY. |
| `R-dependency-tracked` | SETTLED | The system shall track the dependency network between requirements via Requirement.relations (depends_on, refines). |
| `R-deterministic-generation` | SETTLED | The Go generator (internal/generator, surfaced as `hotam gen-spec`) shall produce byte-stable LF utf-8 output with no timestamps or randomness — two runs over an unchanged graph yield identical bytes. |
| `R-director-agent-required-per-domain` | SETTLED | Every domain must contain a `director` agent at `domains/<name>/agents/director/` as the entry point operator. |
| `R-doc-names-reader` | SETTLED | Every generated doc shall name its reader as an existing Stakeholder id in its header. |
| `R-docs-generated-from-requirements` | SETTLED | Per-topic narrative files (atoms-operator.md, atoms-substrate.md, atoms-discipline.md, atoms-check.md) shall be generated from SETTLED requirements grouped by topic, written into the active domain's docs/gen/ by the generator. |
| `R-domain-declares-director` | SETTLED | Every domain's manifest shall declare a DIRECTOR agent that resolves to a real agent directory at `domains/<name>/agents/<DIRECTOR>/`. |
| `R-domain-delegation-as-node` | REJECTED | A domain-delegation shall be recorded as a `Delegation` substrate node with fields parent_op, child_op, scope, border, returns_contract, crystal_path. |
| `R-domain-delegation-persists` | REJECTED | A domain-delegation shall persist as a directory + a substrate node (`Delegation`). |
| `R-domain-founded-in-wave-order` | SETTLED | A new domain MUST be founded general-before-specific, not bottom-up from requirements, in 8 steps (the same authored-executable-specification canvas PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md Section 8 applies to a whole domain): (1) purpose, stakeholders, terminology MUST land first -- manifest purpose/goals plus Stakeholders plus Axes; (2) Process and its scenarios MUST land BEFORE any EntityType -- a Process node naming stages, roles, and drives_entities; (3) the object model, authored by hand in spec/model/ (all aggregates/value-objects and their fields, general before any one model's detail) MUST land BEFORE any operation; (4) operations, policies, and invariants, authored by hand in spec/application/ and spec/policy/, MUST land BEFORE any test; (5) executable tests, authored by hand, MUST land BEFORE the Requirements they prove; (6) Requirements, each linked to that authored code and those tests via implemented_by/verified_by, MUST land BEFORE Assumptions/Conflicts; (7) Assumptions and Conflicts MUST land BEFORE the final step; (8) generated documentation (REQUIREMENTS/PIPELINE/MODELS/TRACEABILITY/COVERAGE.md) and a coverage audit close the founding sequence. Each step MUST close with a regeneration (`hotam gen-spec`) and a check of the overview projection (PIPELINE.md, R-domain-overview-projection) before the next step opens. The diff against the domain's original source prose MUST run AFTER step 1 (purpose/stakeholders/terminology), NEVER only at the end of the founding sequence. |
| `R-domain-has-docs-dir` | SETTLED | Every domains/<name>/ shall contain a docs/ directory which wraps the generated docs/gen/ plus any hand-written domain material. |
| `R-domain-has-manifest` | SETTLED | Every `domains/<name>/` directory contains a `manifest.json` defining the domain's identity fields (id, description, goals, director), so the generator can discover a domain's metadata without loading its full graph. |
| `R-domain-is-a-directory` | SETTLED | A business domain is represented as a directory at `domains/<name>/`. |
| `R-domain-map-generated` | SETTLED | The root `CLAUDE.md` shall contain a DOMAIN-MAP block listing every `domains/<name>/` with id, description, goals, director, path, atoms-count. |
| `R-domain-map-shows-pulse` | SETTLED | The root CLAUDE.md DOMAIN-MAP block shall carry, for every domain, an 'open actions' line stating that domain's open-action count and top action, and emit_cipher shall surface the aggregate open-action count of all non-pinned domains in the injected pulse. |
| `R-domain-overview-projection` | SETTLED | Every domain MUST answer the overview question ("how is this domain structured as a whole") with ONE generated projection (OVERVIEW/PIPELINE.md), assembled from Process nodes plus EntityType plus the pipeline references between entities. This projection ALWAYS speaks in the language of the methodology/business-domain author: stage names are taken verbatim from Process.steps as authored, and any Go/code identifier appears ONLY as an adjacent annotation, never as a replacement for the author's own name. The projection's regenerability is the mechanical completeness test against the domain's original prose description: a stage or concept present in the source prose that does not surface anywhere in the projection is a discoverable coverage defect, not a silent gap. |
| `R-domain-owns-claude-md` | REJECTED | Each `domains/<name>/CLAUDE.md` is the domain-scoped operator-prompt, generated by `gen_spec.py`. |
| `R-domain-owns-docs-gen` | SETTLED | Each `domains/<name>/docs/gen/` shall hold only the markdown generated from that domain's graph, with no cross-domain doc files. |
| `R-domain-owns-graph-py` | SETTLED | Each `domains/<name>/` owns its own graph store (`graph.json`) defining its requirement/conflict/assumption graph, loaded and written via the shared loader. |
| `R-domain-owns-tools-and-agents` | SETTLED | Each `domains/<name>/` shall materialize a `tools/` directory (private tools) and an `agents/` directory (sub-operators) only when a real sub-tool or sub-agent is actually created — not eagerly scaffolded; a domain with no sub-tools/sub-agents yet correctly carries neither directory. |
| `R-domain-self-hosting-flag` | SETTLED | Framework-jurisdiction invariants (frameworkScopedInvariantNames) shall run only against a graph whose manifest declares self_hosting=true. |
| `R-drift-structurally-impossible` | SETTLED | The generated docs/gen/*.md and graph.json shall equal the regeneration of the current graph, byte-for-byte. |
| `R-empty-content-calm-banner` | SETTLED | When the active domain has no content yet (empty graph), `hotam what-now` shall render a calm 'no content yet'-style signal, not an error. |
| `R-empty-content-gen-notice` | SETTLED | When the active domain has no content yet (missing graph), `hotam gen-spec` shall emit a 'no content yet' notice into docs/gen/*.md, not fail. |
| `R-empty-content-is-legitimate` | REJECTED | A freshly-cloned framework with no spec/content/graph.py shall be structurally well-formed; what_now renders a calm 'no content yet' banner and gen_spec emits the same notice. |
| `R-empty-content-wellformed` | SETTLED | A freshly-cloned framework with an empty graph shall pass all structural invariants â€” an empty graph is well-formed. |
| `R-enforceability-kind-declared` | SETTLED | A requirement shall carry an enforceability kind from the set ENFORCEABLE or INHERENTLY_PROSE, distinguishing real closeable debt from permanent discipline. |
| `R-enforced-by-resolvable` | SETTLED | Every SETTLED/ENFORCED requirement's enforced_by entries shall resolve to a concrete enforcer — a registered check_* invariant or a real top-level Test* function under internal/**/*_test.go — via the shared enforcer-resolution logic. |
| `R-enforced-names-enforcer` | SETTLED | An ENFORCED requirement shall name its enforcing invariant or test in enforced_by. |
| `R-enforcement-first-class` | SETTLED | The enforcement level (PROSE / STRUCTURAL / ENFORCED) shall be a first-class Requirement field with enforced_by anchors naming the check_* invariant or Test* function that guarantees the claim. |
| `R-enforcement-gradient` | REJECTED | A requirement shall carry an enforcement level PROSE \| STRUCTURAL \| ENFORCED, and ENFORCED requirements shall name their enforcing invariant/test. |
| `R-enforcement-levels-declared` | SETTLED | A requirement shall carry an enforcement level from the set PROSE, STRUCTURAL, ENFORCED. |
| `R-enforcement-perimeter-baselines-guarded` | SETTLED | A host-hook guard shall deny direct Edit/Write to enforcement-perimeter baseline files (ratchet-test baselines, the active-domain pin), with sanctioned updates routed through a dedicated baseline-update tool. |
| `R-enforcement-perimeter-visible` | SETTLED | A content-hash pin shall cover the enforcement-perimeter code files, failing RED on any content change until the baseline is consciously updated via a dedicated baseline-update tool. |
| `R-entities-md-generated` | SETTLED | domains/<name>/docs/gen/ENTITIES.md shall be generated from the domain's graph by gen-spec, listing every EntityType with its lifecycle Mermaid diagram, fields, covering check_entity_* invariants, and instances. |
| `R-entity-checks-by-iteration` | SETTLED | The check_entity_* invariant family shall cover every declared EntityType by iterating g.EntityTypes, requiring no new check_* code per additional type. |
| `R-entity-derived-requirement` | SETTLED | Each EntityType in the domain's graph shall be projected as R-entity-<slug> in the domain's FRAMEWORK-INVARIANTS.md, with enforced_by listing the check_entity_* family covering it. |
| `R-entity-field-kind-known` | SETTLED | Every EntityField.kind shall be in ENTITY_FIELD_KINDS (string \| number \| enum \| reference \| state). |
| `R-entity-instance-id-prefix` | SETTLED | Every EntityInstance.id shall start with 'ENT-<entity_type>-' (typed-anchor discipline). |
| `R-entity-instance-refs-resolve` | SETTLED | Every EntityInstance reference field shall resolve in the graph according to its ref_target. |
| `R-entity-instance-required-fields` | SETTLED | Every EntityInstance shall provide values for all EntityFields with required=True. |
| `R-entity-instance-state-in-lifecycle` | SETTLED | Every EntityInstance.state shall be a valid state in the corresponding EntityType.lifecycle. |
| `R-entity-is-declarative` | SETTLED | The framework shall supply no built-in EntityType values â€” all entity types are declared by domains in build_graph(). |
| `R-entity-reuses-lifecycle` | SETTLED | Each EntityType.Lifecycle shall be a Lifecycle value (the §Lifecycle keystone) with no parallel state machinery introduced. |
| `R-entity-state-conflict-surfaced` | SETTLED | Two processes driving one EntityType to disjoint terminal or quiescent states shall surface as a P5 LATENT_CONNECTOR action via entity_state_conflict_suspects(). |
| `R-entity-type-lifecycle-wellformed` | SETTLED | Every EntityType.lifecycle shall be a well-formed Lifecycle validated by check_entity_type_lifecycle_wellformed. |
| `R-entity-typed-anchors` | SETTLED | check_typed_anchors shall validate the ENT- prefix for EntityInstance nodes. |
| `R-framework-claude-md-is-domain-free` | REJECTED | The root `CLAUDE.md` shall contain only framework-level content, referencing domains exclusively through the DOMAIN-MAP block. |
| `R-framework-owned-by-no-agent` | SETTLED | The framework body (`internal/` package tree) shall be owned by no single agent -- it is shared infrastructure any agent's code may import. |
| `R-framework-shared-docs-generated` | SETTLED | The framework shall generate thinking/*.md and tools/*.md deterministically from the methodology section registry and tool registry (the single source of truth). |
| `R-framework-suite-domain-independent` | SETTLED | The framework test subset shall pass green under ANY active domain, or none, independent of which business domain is pinned. |
| `R-framework-suite-tiered` | SETTLED | The test suite shall partition every collected test into exactly one of two responsibility tiers -- 'framework' (exercising internal/ mechanics) or 'domain' (asserting concrete self-domain content) -- so the framework tier is a separately selectable, self-contained subset. |
| `R-gate-signoff-single-carrier` | SETTLED | A domain that runs its Requirements through a staged review methodology (e.g. prat/gpsm-sm's P-G0..P-G4 planning gates) has ONE typed carrier on the graph recording per-Requirement, per-stage gate passage -- Requirement.gate_signoffs -- instead of that fact being reconstructed from prose scattered across History entries, manifest goals, and Process why-fields (a drift that has already recurred once in this framework's own consumer domains). Within one Requirement and one pipeline_run, SIGNED gate_signoffs MUST appear in the domain-declared gate_stage_order (a manifest.json opt-in DATA field, never an engine-known enum -- the engine serves domains with no staged-gate discipline at all) -- no stage SIGNED before every earlier stage is SIGNED too. Every DEFERRED gate_signoff MUST carry a non-empty deferred_reason, and when that reason references a Conflict id (C-[0-9a-f]{8}), that id MUST resolve to a real Conflict node in the graph. |
| `R-generations-inherit-doc-test-code` | SETTLED | Every artifact the methodology generates MUST recursively inherit the doc-test-code form: EVERY EntityType MUST yield a Go struct, its lifecycle methods, and transition tests; EVERY SETTLED requirement MUST yield a named Go test case (a real assertion when formalizable, an honest skip carrying the rule text otherwise). |
| `R-glossary-drift-stable` | SETTLED | The committed docs/gen/GLOSSARY.md shall equal the regeneration of the current graph byte-for-byte. |
| `R-glossary-generated` | SETTLED | A controlled vocabulary of methodology terms shall be generated under docs/gen/GLOSSARY.md. |
| `R-glossary-sync-fails-dead` | SETTLED | The glossary sync test shall fail when a defined vocabulary term is not used anywhere in the framework. |
| `R-glossary-sync-fails-unused` | SETTLED | The glossary sync test shall fail when a section-anchor token used in the framework is absent from the glossary. |
| `R-glossary-sync-test` | REJECTED | A controlled vocabulary of methodology terms shall be generated under docs/gen/GLOSSARY.md, with a sync test that fails on undefined or unused terms. |
| `R-goal-as-target-state` | REJECTED | A Goal shall be a desired target-state predicate; the Gap = (Goal - current state) is the work that drives a Process. |
| `R-goal-is-first-class-type` | SETTLED | Goal shall be its own first-class struct type (not a Requirement facet) with typed anchor 'GOAL-'. |
| `R-goal-owner-is-operator` | SETTLED | Goal.owner shall reference an existing Operator.id. |
| `R-goal-target-kind-known` | SETTLED | Goal.target_state.kind shall be one of the declared TARGET_KINDS. |
| `R-goal-type-vs-facet` | SETTLED | Goal shall be its own first-class struct type (not a Requirement facet), with typed anchor 'GOAL-' and its own GoalLifecycle. |
| `R-held-carries-variants` | SETTLED | A HELD Conflict shall carry at least two elaborated behavior Variants (id, behavior, implies, costs) as a payload field, not as new graph nodes. |
| `R-history-from-rejected-markers` | REJECTED | docs/gen/HISTORY.md shall be generated from REJECTED markers in requirement WHY blocks and from DECIDED/REVISIT_WHEN lifecycle states on Conflicts. |
| `R-history-generated-from-decided` | SETTLED | docs/gen/HISTORY.md shall include entries generated from DECIDED and REVISIT_WHEN lifecycle states on Conflicts. |
| `R-history-generated-from-rejected` | SETTLED | docs/gen/HISTORY.md shall include entries generated from REJECTED markers in requirement WHY blocks. |
| `R-initiator-supplies-domain-content` | SETTLED | An agent shall receive its domain content from its initiator at boot and crystallize it into the domain code-spec (graph.json). |
| `R-land-gate-tier-selector` | SETTLED | hotam gate (internal/gate.SelectTier1), when invoked for a target anchor, shall resolve the T1 targeted-enforcer subset from the target's enforced_by tuple (union of resolved check_*/Test* names) instead of requiring the full test suite for that lookup. |
| `R-land-gate-tier-selector-fails-closed` | SETTLED | internal/gate.SelectTier1 shall fall back to Confident=false (fail-closed, signaling the caller to run the full suite) on any selection uncertainty: an unknown/unresolvable target anchor, an empty enforced_by tuple, a Conflict target (no per-instance enforced_by), or any enforced_by entry that cannot be resolved to a concrete Go check_*/Test* function -- never returning a partial or best-effort subset in an uncertain case. |
| `R-land-is-transactional` | SETTLED | hotam land shall leave a domain's graph.json and generated docs mutually consistent even when a later stage (doc regeneration or the post-regen violations check) fails after the proposal apply stage already wrote a new graph.json — a failure rolls back to the pre-land state rather than leaving the graph and docs divergent. |
| `R-land-tier-trace` | SETTLED | Every applied proposal that reaches the LAND verify step shall append its verification tier (T1 targeted or T2 full-suite), selected test node-ids (or the literal 'full'), and verify/closure outcome to a runtime land-log, written AFTER the verify step so the record states what actually ran. |
| `R-land-tier-trace-best-effort` | SETTLED | A broken or unwritable spec/.runtime/land-log.jsonl location shall never fail an otherwise-green apply -- the write is best-effort, warn only. |
| `R-land-tier-trace-skips-dry-run` | SETTLED | A dry-run proposal shall never write a spec/.runtime/land-log.jsonl record. |
| `R-latent-connectors-cluster-by-assumption` | SETTLED | The what-now harness shall render latent-connector suspects as one P5 action per shared-assumption cluster rather than one action per requirement pair. |
| `R-lifecycle-abstraction` | REJECTED | A generic hotam_spec.lifecycle (State / Transition / Lifecycle) shall be introduced; Requirement.status and Conflict.lifecycle shall validate against framework-supplied Lifecycle constants. |
| `R-lifecycle-validates-conflict` | SETTLED | Conflict.lifecycle shall validate against the framework-supplied CONFLICT_LIFECYCLE. |
| `R-lifecycle-validates-goal` | SETTLED | Goal.lifecycle shall validate against the framework-supplied GOAL_LIFECYCLE. |
| `R-lifecycle-validates-operator` | SETTLED | Operator.lifecycle shall validate against the framework-supplied OPERATOR_LIFECYCLE. |
| `R-lifecycle-validates-requirement` | SETTLED | Requirement.status shall validate against the framework-supplied REQUIREMENT_STATUS_LIFECYCLE. |
| `R-m-tag-format-valid` | SETTLED | Every Requirement.m_tag (when non-empty) shall match `^M[1-9][0-9]*$`, be unique across the graph, and appear only on OPEN requirements. |
| `R-machine-check-syntactic` | SETTLED | Every non-empty Assumption.machine_check shall be a well-formed Python expression (compilable in eval mode), never free prose. |
| `R-measure-context-size` | REJECTED | The operator's working-context fullness shall be MEASURED from a runtime stamp, not estimated, so the three-cipher pulse cites a real number. |
| `R-method-matches-docstring` | SETTLED | Each check_* function registered in the invariant registry shall carry a Claim/Rule/Why text that shares non-trivial lexical overlap with its Violation messages. |
| `R-no-observation-type` | SETTLED | The ontology (internal/ontology) shall define no Observation or Evidence struct type anywhere -- Assumption remains the ontology's sole belief-carrying node type. |
| `R-observation-evidence-scope` | SETTLED | Operator epistemics (observations, beliefs, reasoning) shall live in the working dialogue, crystallized into the substrate only on request. |
| `R-open-states-question` | SETTLED | Every requirement whose status begins with 'OPEN' shall carry a non-empty question of the form OPEN(<question>). |
| `R-open-tickets-visible` | SETTLED | The what-now harness shall surface a CLI-only band summarising open (non-done) on-disk tickets broken down by status, read from the filesystem and never fed into DiagnoseSignals. |
| `R-operator-acting-facet` | REJECTED | An Operator shall be a Stakeholder's ACTING facet: it owns a bounded DomainScope, carries a ContextBudget and capabilities, and may have a parent Operator. |
| `R-operator-backend-protocol` | REJECTED | The framework's tools shall talk to the acting agent through a single OperatorBackend protocol (get_context_state / request_resolver_approval / delegate), so the methodology does not hard-depend on which coding-agent or model drives it. |
| `R-operator-crystal-embeds-thinking` | REJECTED | The operator's CLAUDE.md shall embed the full content of its scope-relevant thinking documentation inline, not as markdown links, so the operator holds the methodology itself rather than a table of contents. |
| `R-operator-crystal-embeds-thinking-distilled` | SETTLED | The operator's CLAUDE.md shall embed one RULE sentence per thinking topic (sourced from Section.Canon, guarded via shortForm) plus a path link to domains/<domain>/docs/gen/thinking/<slug>.md, not a multi-line RULE+WHY distillate. |
| `R-operator-crystal-embeds-tools` | REJECTED | The operator's CLAUDE.md shall embed the full content of its scope-relevant tool documentation inline, not as markdown links. |
| `R-operator-crystal-embeds-tools-distilled` | SETTLED | The operator's CLAUDE.md shall embed an EMBEDDED-TOOLS block that reports the Implemented and Planned tool counts from the methodology.Tool registry and directs the operator to `hotam -h`, `hotam status --json`, `hotam req`, `hotam brief`, and docs/gen/tools/INDEX.md for on-demand detail — a compact pointer-only reference, not a per-tool distillation. |
| `R-operator-crystal-is-claude-md` | REJECTED | Each operator's crystallized substrate shall be its own CLAUDE.md — an anchored map of its bounded sub-domain that it reloads BY REFERENCE rather than re-carrying; the director-operator's CLAUDE.md holds the overall graph and references each sub-operator's CLAUDE.md. |
| `R-operator-has-context-budget` | SETTLED | An Operator shall carry a ContextBudget with a positive limit and a declared measure. |
| `R-operator-is-frozen-dataclass` | SETTLED | An Operator shall be a dedicated struct type in internal/ontology/operator.go carrying the typed anchor 'OP-', with field mutations performed only through the proposal system. |
| `R-operator-may-have-parent` | SETTLED | An Operator.parent shall reference another Operator.id or be None (root). |
| `R-operator-not-self-approve` | SETTLED | An Operator shall not resolver a Conflict in which its underlying Stakeholder owns one of the members. |
| `R-operator-prompt-from-substrate` | SETTLED | The operator-prompt CLAUDE.md shall include a CONSTITUTION block (compact per-category summary of SETTLED requirements) generated deterministically from the active domain's graph.json, with the full id+claim roster in docs/gen/CONSTITUTION.md. |
| `R-operator-prompt-loaded-at-session-start` | SETTLED | A SessionStart hook shall run `hotam gen-spec` before the operator's first turn of any session, ensuring root CLAUDE.md is current substrate-derived state. |
| `R-operator-references-stakeholder` | SETTLED | An Operator.stakeholder shall reference an existing Stakeholder.id. |
| `R-operator-type-vs-facet` | SETTLED | Operator shall be its own first-class struct type in internal/ontology/operator.go (not a Stakeholder facet), with typed anchor 'OP-', a ContextBudget, and an optional parent reference. |
| `R-orientation-faq-answerable` | SETTLED | An AI agent (or a new human reader) orients in this project fast: for every basic onboarding question a domain DECLARES in its own manifest.json "orientation_faq" opt-in list (e.g. what is this project / what is the requirement lifecycle / who decides what / what is currently blocked / where is the full requirements list), the answer MUST be reachable from the domain's generated crystal in at most ONE hop -- NEVER buried behind a chain of pointers the agent would have to follow blind. An answer is reachable in <=1 hop when EITHER (a) the manifest entry's declared keywords ALL appear inline in the crystal's text (the answer is present with ZERO hops), OR (b) the manifest entry's declared link (a repo-root-relative path, the SAME convention the crystal's own cross-references use) appears in the crystal's text (as a markdown link OR a bare path) AND resolves to a REAL EXISTING FILE on disk. A domain that has NOT declared an orientation_faq list makes NO orientation promise and this requirement is an honest no-op for it -- exactly the same opt-in boundary discipline:"full" / the committed SPEC.md / the vendored recorder already draw between the engine's structural floor and a domain's own stricter, explicitly-declared contract. |
| `R-overlap-single-presenter` | SETTLED | Every node contested by two or more operators' overlapping scope projections shall resolve to exactly one deterministic presenter. |
| `R-parallel-mutating-agents-use-worktree` | SETTLED | Parallel agent invocations that mutate tracked repository files shall use git worktree isolation unless their target files are provably disjoint and no history-rewriting git operation is planned during their execution window. |
| `R-partition-vs-border` | REJECTED | Operator sub-domains shall relate to the parent graph by a single declared discipline (strict partition or declared-border overlap). |
| `R-per-node-json-store` | REJECTED | Each graph node (Requirement/Conflict/Assumption/...) shall be persisted in its own separate JSON file, with a schema validating each file on load, instead of living in the single graph.py. |
| `R-post-compact-regen-from-substrate` | SETTLED | A PostCompact hook shall run `hotam gen-spec` after every auto-compact so the post-compact prompt reload reads fresh substrate-derived CLAUDE.md, not the stale pre-compact version. |
| `R-prefer-tool-over-hand` | SETTLED | The operator shall prefer a reusable tool over performing the same action by hand, with one-off acts permitted only for genuine bootstrap or single-occurrence events. |
| `R-presented-pending-decision-type` | SETTLED | Proposals shall be organized under wave-numbered folders in `proposals/` (`waveN-<slug>/`, optionally with batch subfolders), which serves the pending-vs-applied practical need without a dedicated `pending/`/`applied/` runtime split or a new graph node type; a proposal file lives in its wave folder from the TRANSLATE step through LAND. |
| `R-private-tools-in-agent-folder` | SETTLED | Tools available only to one agent shall live under that agent's tools/ subdirectory (domains/<domain>/agents/<name>/tools/). |
| `R-process-aspect-first` | REJECTED | hotam_spec.process shall be the FIRST opt-in behavioral aspect — Lifecycle + Steps + roles_required + drives_entities — added after the keystone Lifecycle abstraction lands. |
| `R-process-drives-existing-entities` | SETTLED | Every entity slug referenced in a Process.drives_entities shall resolve to a declared EntityType slug in g.entity_types. |
| `R-process-goal-owner-is-operator-aspect` | SETTLED | Every Goal.owner shall reference an existing Operator.id, validated by check_goal_owner_is_operator. |
| `R-process-lifecycle-wellformed-aspect` | SETTLED | Every Process node shall have a well-formed lifecycle validated by check_process_lifecycle_wellformed. |
| `R-process-opt-in` | SETTLED | The Process aspect shall be opt-in: TensionGraph.processes defaults to an empty tuple. |
| `R-process-roles-declared-aspect` | SETTLED | Every role referenced in a Process step shall be declared in the Process roles_required tuple. |
| `R-process-typed-anchors-extended` | SETTLED | check_typed_anchors shall validate PR- and GOAL- prefixes for Process and Goal nodes. |
| `R-process-types-exist` | SETTLED | internal/ontology/process.go shall define Process, Step, Goal, TargetState structs, plus ProcessLifecycle and GoalLifecycle canonical lifecycle variables. |
| `R-project-name-hotam-spec` | SETTLED | The project's name shall be Hotam-Spec (display) and hotam-spec (kebab-case for domain-level artifacts — domain directory names like `domains/hotam-spec-self`, the `hotam` CLI binary name, and repository slug conventions), while the Go module path shall match the real git remote and is deliberately `github.com/PHPCraftdream/HotamSpec` (PascalCase suffix, per commit 4325ac8), closing M1. |
| `R-project-root-not-hardcoded` | SETTLED | HotamSpec resolves the consumer's project root through a single function, paths.ProjectRoot() (internal/paths/project_root.go), via a documented R1-R6 priority chain, never through a raw runtime.Caller-based parents-climb guess at the consumer's files. |
| `R-proposed-conflict-kind-exists` | SETTLED | The proposal protocol shall include a ProposedConflict kind (kind='Conflict') that materializes a new Conflict node in the active domain's graph via `hotam apply-proposal`. |
| `R-proposed-stakeholder-kind-exists` | SETTLED | The proposal protocol shall include a ProposedStakeholder kind (Kind="Stakeholder") that materializes a new Stakeholder node in the active domain's graph by appending to g.Stakeholders, refusing a duplicate id. |
| `R-rdf-store` | REJECTED | The tension graph shall be persisted in an RDF triplestore with SHACL shapes and SPARQL traversal. |
| `R-recently-rejected-surfaced` | SETTLED | Root CLAUDE.md shall contain a RECENTLY-REJECTED sentinel block listing every REJECTED requirement whose why contains 'REJECTED — REPLACES' to surface anti-relitigation evidence before re-derivation. |
| `R-reflection-predicates-first-class` | SETTLED | The operator's self-diagnosis conditions shall be named, pure, graph-only predicate functions in internal/diagnose/finding.go composed by AllFindings in a fixed registry order, and consumed by the what-now harness via internal/diagnose/signal.go -- never inlined in tool code. |
| `R-rejected-preserved-not-deleted` | SETTLED | Requirements that are rejected shall be marked REJECTED and kept in the graph for history, never deleted. |
| `R-repo-map-generated` | REJECTED | CLAUDE.md shall contain a REPO-MAP block listing every spec/src/hotam_spec/*.py, spec/tools/*.py, docs/gen/*.md, and spec/content/*.py with a one-line role from its module docstring or front matter. |
| `R-requirement-claim-is-atomic` | SETTLED | Each Requirement.claim shall assert exactly one concern, with conjunctions of distinct concerns decomposed into separate requirements. |
| `R-requirement-enforced` | SETTLED | A SETTLED requirement that names no enforcing invariant or test is UNENFORCED (claimed-but-not-guaranteed, soft context-debt). |
| `R-resolver-distinct-from-owners` | SETTLED | Every Conflict's resolver shall be a Stakeholder who is NOT the owner of any of the conflict's members. |
| `R-review-mark-carries-evidence` | SETTLED | A ProposedReviewMark proposal shall fail validation unless it carries at least one non-whitespace evidence entry. |
| `R-review-mark-requires-substantive-review` | SETTLED | A SETTLED requirement's last_reviewed_at shall be updated only after its claim/why/evidence/source_refs have actually been read and checked against current code/graph reality; a blanket date backfill with no substantive check is not a review and must not set last_reviewed_at. |
| `R-revisit-markers-evaluated` | SETTLED | The what-now harness shall surface a CLI-only action for each DECIDED conflict whose revisit_marker has never been evaluated or was last evaluated more than the staleness delta of SETTLED atoms ago. |
| `R-root-claude-md-contains-domain-crystal` | REJECTED | Root CLAUDE.md shall embed the full content of the active domain's CLAUDE.md inside a DOMAIN-CRYSTAL sentinel block generated by gen_spec.py. |
| `R-root-claude-md-is-sentinel-only` | SETTLED | The root CLAUDE.md (and its AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md siblings) shall be rendered by gen-spec from a template whose body is a minimal framework-identity header plus sentinel-bounded generated blocks, with no hand-written prose between sentinels. |
| `R-rules-as-data` | SETTLED | Regular invariant families (homogeneous per-entity structural checks such as dangling-refs, typed-anchors, and lifecycle-membership) shall be CLASSIFIED as table-driven data distinct from irreducibly bespoke invariants (identity derivation, cross-entity bijections, docstring/body coherence, budget arithmetic) -- with derivation of check_* function bodies from any such table explicitly deferred. |
| `R-run-speed-guarded` | SETTLED | Test-run duration shall not silently degrade: a self-calibrating guard (baseline = mean of the first 5 local runs * 1.2, stored per-machine off-git) fails the suite when a run exceeds the baseline. |
| `R-scenario-spec-obligations-mechanically-enforced` | SETTLED | PLAN-scenario-generated-spec.md §3 W2 (the obligatoriness wave) makes the scenario-generated-spec layer's obligations mechanically real rather than aspirational, and each of its four gates shall be anchored here as the claim it enforces: check_settled_requires_scenario (in a domain whose manifest declares discipline:"full", every SETTLED requirement carries a real carrier -- enforced_by OR implemented_by+verified_by+at-least-one-scenario-narrated verified_by test; an honest no-op for every domain that has not opted in); check_scenario_executes_impl (for every authored-path requirement with implemented_by AND verified_by both non-empty, regardless of discipline:full, every implemented_by symbol is actually executed by at least one verified_by test under real `go test -coverprofile` instrumentation); check_spec_md_current (a committed docs/gen/SPEC.md, when present, is byte-identical to a fresh `hotam gen-spec --spec` run; an honest no-op for a domain with no SPEC.md yet); and check_model_complete (in a discipline:"full" domain, every authored model object with at least one exported method cited as implemented_by is COMPLETE, each cited method backed by a scenario-narrated verified_by test). Each gate is an honest no-op before its own opt-in trigger fires (no discipline:full, no SPEC.md, no authored-path pair), so the migration is per-domain and carries no long-lived opt-out flag. |
| `R-scope-is-projection` | SETTLED | An operator's sub-domain shall be a computed PROJECTION (an id-set view derived by prefix match over the shared graph), never a copy of any node. |
| `R-scope-overlap-generated` | SETTLED | When two operators' scope projections share a node, the overlap shall be computed (never hidden or silently merged); the rendering of that overlap into a per-operator crystal is deferred until a real second operator exists. |
| `R-seed-in-src` | REJECTED | The framework shall ship with a seed graph baked into spec/src/hotam_spec/graph.py so the demo runs without setup. |
| `R-sensorium-committed` | SETTLED | The universal sensorium hooks (SessionStart/PostCompact gen_spec, UserPromptSubmit emit_cipher+claude_md_diff_watch+attention_hook, PreToolUse graph-guard, Stop context_producer) shall live in a committed project settings.json generated by a setup tool with commands portable via $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, never only in the personal git-ignored settings.local.json. |
| `R-setup-claude-generates-settings` | REJECTED | The file `.claude/settings.json` shall be generated by `spec/tools/setup_claude.py` deterministically; hand-edits are forbidden by a meta-test. |
| `R-shared-thinking-doc-from-canon-sections` | SETTLED | Each spec/docs/thinking/<topic-slug>.md shall be generated as the union of all framework docstrings carrying Canon: §<Topic> markers, never hand-written. |
| `R-shared-tool-doc-from-docstring-and-help` | SETTLED | Each docs/gen/tools/<command>.md shall be a pure function of that tool's methodology.Tool registry entry (Command/Canon/Purpose/Status), generated wholesale by BuildToolDocs with no hand-written content. |
| `R-shared-tools-in-spec-tools` | SETTLED | Tools available to all operators shall be the single shared hotam CLI toolset -- Go subcommands wired in cmd/hotam/main.go and declared once in the methodology.Tools registry -- with no per-agent private tool namespace. |
| `R-signoff-preserved-in-substrate` | SETTLED | A resolver signoff on a DECIDED/HELD Conflict or a transitioned Assumption shall be preserved as a Signoff payload IN the graph node (not only in gitignored proposal JSON) -- decided_by, date, verbatim (optional), instrument and chosen_variant (for HELD->DECIDED) are auditable from the substrate. |
| `R-smoke-test` | SETTLED | The test suite shall provide a fast end-to-end signal that the framework is healthy on the real domain graph -- load the graph, run every generator template, and regenerate docs without panicking or empty output. |
| `R-spawn-log-carries-isolation` | SETTLED | Every spawn-log entry shall carry isolation (worktree\|shared) and mutating (bool) fields, defaulting to shared/false when the caller omits them. |
| `R-speak-by-reference` | SETTLED | An operator shall communicate by reference, ensuring every assertion cites at least one concrete anchor in the info-space. |
| `R-speak-domain-register-by-default` | SETTLED | An operator ALWAYS speaks in the language of the LAYER it is currently working in, and AVOIDS the language of any HIGHER layer whenever the same thing can be said in the current layer's own terms. The layers, bottom-up, are: (1) the active CONSUMER domain's own language -- its business concepts, names, stages, and roles (e.g. for gsm: FT numbers, P-G0..P-G4 gates, PM/BA/TechLead roles, the essence of a conflict or stage); (2) the methodology-constitution's own terms (the prat layer); (3) the Hotam engine's internals (the graph, R-/C-/A-/OP- anchors, SETTLED/ENFORCED/DRAFT statuses, check_* invariants, `hotam` commands). Escalation to a higher layer's language is permitted ONLY when: (a) the human explicitly asks to switch to that layer, OR (b) the human's own message already uses that higher layer's terms. Absent both conditions, the agent stays at the current layer's language even when it could formally reference something higher; the first answer leads with the essence of the current layer, NEVER with the name of the Hotam framework. R-speak-by-reference stays in force for the TRANSLATE/PRESENT/LAND steps of the mediation loop, where anchor-citation is mandatory to the act itself; this requirement governs the DEFAULT register of every other answer. |
| `R-speculative-aspects-frozen` | SETTLED | The Entity aspect, multi-domain federation, and sub-agent recursion machinery shall receive no inward development while frozen, unfreezing only when a real business domain demonstrates concrete need. |
| `R-stable-conflict-identity` | SETTLED | A Conflict's id shall equal conflict_identity(axis, context) — the deterministic hash of its tension, not its members. |
| `R-stale-substrate` | SETTLED | Crystallized knowledge whose enforcing assumption has died shall be surfaced as stale (enforced-but-wrong, a bad habit). |
| `R-statemachine-deterministic` | SETTLED | A Lifecycle's transitions shall be deterministic — no two transitions with the same (src, event) and overlapping guards. |
| `R-statemachine-guard-on-assumption` | SETTLED | A Transition.guard may name an Assumption it rests on (drift seam) — when that Assumption dies, the guard is surfaced. |
| `R-statemachine-reachable` | SETTLED | Every state in a canonical Lifecycle shall be reachable from the initial state. |
| `R-statemachine-terminal-or-cyclic` | SETTLED | Every non-cyclic Lifecycle shall reach at least one terminal/quiescent state. |
| `R-statemachine-wellformedness` | REJECTED | Every modeled state machine shall be reachable, deterministic, and terminal (or explicitly cyclic); a transition guard may rest on an Assumption (the behavioral drift seam). |
| `R-status-single-command-summary` | SETTLED | The hotam CLI shall provide a `status` command (`hotam status [--domain <path>] [--today YYYY-MM-DD] [--json]`) that composes what-now's top action + debt counts (internal/diagnose), due's freshness counts (internal/freshness), and all-violations' violation count (internal/invariants) into a single compact summary, so an agent does not need to run those three commands separately to reconstruct the same picture. It reuses the same underlying functions those commands call rather than reimplementing their logic, never gates (exit code always 0), and its --json output uses flat, explicitly named fields (top_action, settled_count, enforced_count, closeable_debt_count, overdue_count, never_reviewed_count, violation_count, node_count) parseable in one shot. |
| `R-step-invokes-known-transition` | SETTLED | Every Step.transition (when non-empty) shall name a transition event declared in the driven EntityType.lifecycle. |
| `R-sub-agent-crystal-triad` | SETTLED | Every sub-agent's CLAUDE.md shall contain three parts: scope-filtered embedded methodology thinking, a reference to its parent/senior agent, and its own scope-filtered domain business (CONSTITUTION). |
| `R-subagent-gets-its-claude-md` | SETTLED | A delegated sub-operator shall receive its OWN crystal, a CLAUDE.md generated from its sub-domain. |
| `R-t1-selector-as-land-default` | DRAFT | hotam land shall, by default, invoke internal/gate.SelectTier1 for its target anchor and run only the resolved T1 enforcer subset (falling back to the full suite on any selector uncertainty, per R-land-gate-tier-selector-fails-closed's contract) instead of relying solely on all-violations, so each proposal apply pays only the verification cost its own enforced_by tuple demands. |
| `R-task-spawn-is-a-hand` | SETTLED | A task-agent invocation (a sh/Agent-tool call) is a hand -- a one-shot delegated act, not a standing sub-operator. |
| `R-task-spawn-is-ephemeral` | REJECTED | A task-agent invocation (a sh/Agent-tool call) is a hand: it returns conclusions and does not persist between invocations. |
| `R-task-spawn-log-runtime` | SETTLED | The spawn_agent tool shall append a spawn-log entry to .runtime/spawn-log.jsonl -- with parent, child kind, task subject, and stamp -- on every invocation. |
| `R-task-vs-action-distinct-altitudes` | SETTLED | The methodology's Task node type (a modeled work item) and the harness's Action (a fix-the-graph instruction) shall remain distinct types at distinct altitudes — never merged. |
| `R-tension-audit-presents-only` | SETTLED | The deterministic tension-shortlist tool shall never mutate any domain graph.json: its only outputs are a printed shortlist and an advisory recommendation, and every surfaced pair is a SUSPECT for AI/resolver review, never a decided conflict. |
| `R-tension-audit-shortlist-tool` | SETTLED | A tool shall emit a deterministic, LLM-free shortlist of SETTLED requirement pairs that might hide an unmediated tension. |
| `R-tension-audit-staleness-visible` | SETTLED | The what_now harness shall surface a CLI-only action on the 'generative-audit' meter when the tension audit has never run or the live SETTLED graph has grown by more than GENERATIVE_AUDIT_STALE_DELTA atoms since the last recorded sweep. |
| `R-three-cipher-pulse-structurally-injected` | SETTLED | The three-cipher pulse (top action / debt / context) shall be structurally present in the LIVE-STATE block of the root CLAUDE.md, regenerated on every gen-spec / land, so the operator's per-turn ORIENT step reads it by reference rather than re-deriving it. |
| `R-ticket-carries-history` | SETTLED | Every ticket shall carry an append-only ## History section in which each mutation (create, status move, comment, text change) records one machine-recognisable entry, with a text change snapshotting the prior text. |
| `R-ticket-engine-on-disk` | SETTLED | Work items shall be tracked as durable on-disk tickets under tickets/<status>/T-<n>.md, each a JSON-frontmatter header plus a Markdown body, created and moved between status folders by the ticket_* tools. |
| `R-ticket-mutation-via-tools-only` | SETTLED | A ticket's frontmatter header and History shall be changed only through the ticket_* tools, never by hand-editing the file. |
| `R-tiered-gate-not-a-commit-gate` | SETTLED | The full go test ./... suite shall remain the mandatory verification gate at wave and commit boundaries -- the T1 targeted-enforcer tier (hotam gate / internal/gate.SelectTier1) is a standalone advisory selection tool, never a substitute for the full-suite run a resolver or wave-closing agent performs before committing. |
| `R-tool-is-its-own-requirement` | SETTLED | Every tool registered in the methodology.Tools registry whose entry opens with a Canon: §<topic> marker shall be projected into a STRUCTURAL tool-derived requirement R-tool-<basename> with that tool's claim text, enforced by its named enforcer test when one exists. |
| `R-tools-registry-generated` | SETTLED | The list of available tools shall be generated from a single methodology.Tools registry (internal/methodology), never hand-maintained in prose -- each tool carries its Canon section, purpose, and Implemented/Planned status, and the EMBEDDED-TOOLS block of CLAUDE.md is rendered from it. |
| `R-tree-of-crystals-cognitive-trigger` | REJECTED | Tree-of-crystals delegation shall fire when a sub-domain's detail granularity exceeds the director's altitude, independently of the φ-cap size trigger. |
| `R-trust-anchor-delegation-explicit-only` | SETTLED | Delegation of the resolver's personal-signature duty to an agent shall be valid ONLY when granted EXPLICITLY -- per-case or for a declared campaign in advance -- never implied or standing by default. |
| `R-trust-anchor-mechanism` | SETTLED | Every decision shall be personally signed by the human resolver -- today: a decided_by: Stakeholder.id field on the DECIDED Conflict plus git commit authorship. |
| `R-two-altitude-ontology` | SETTLED | The methodology shall use ONE ontology at two altitudes: operator is to the methodology as actor is to the business (the methodology plane is the business plane applied reflexively). |
| `R-uncertain-assumptions-surface` | SETTLED | The what_now harness shall surface every UNCERTAIN assumption carrying at least UNCERTAIN_AGING_MIN_DEPENDENTS dependent requirements as one P4 OPEN_ITEM action asking the resolver to resolve the doubt. |
| `R-uncrystallizable-automated` | SETTLED | Detection of 'uncrystallizable knowledge = missing type' is human judgment: the operator records the candidate in the graph as an OPEN requirement (or a DRAFT), and the resolver decides whether to add the ontology type. |
| `R-uncrystallizable-is-missing-type` | SETTLED | Knowledge an operator cannot crystallize as any existing node shall be RECORDED as a candidate missing ontology type for resolver review (not auto-acted). |
| `R-unmeasured-cipher-names-host-boundary` | SETTLED | While the context cipher is UNMEASURED, the generated LIVE-STATE shall honestly explain that measuring working-context requires host cooperation the framework will not touch, naming no command-to-call. |
| `R-unmeasured-cipher-names-user-action` | REJECTED | While the context cipher is UNMEASURED, the generated LIVE-STATE shall name the exact user-run command that activates measurement. |
| `R-unresolvable-classified-by-human` | SETTLED | Classifying a Conflict as unresolvable-by-amending-its-members shall be a human judgment, never an automated AI inference. |
| `R-unresolvable-conflict-carries-variants` | REJECTED | A Conflict unresolvable by amending its member requirements shall carry at least two elaborated behavior variants; the operator presents the variants, the resolver chooses. |
| `R-user-request-decomposed-to-tickets` | SETTLED | The operator shall, immediately on receiving a user request, decompose it into tickets in the dialogue and ask the addressee -- session tasks or the ticket engine -- before beginning any work. |
| `R-variant-choice-is-decision` | SETTLED | A derived Requirement shall be spawned from a HELD Conflict only after the resolver's ConflictTransition names the chosen Variant, moving the conflict from HELD to DECIDED. |
| `R-vendored-recorder-matches-engine-canon` | SETTLED | A domain's vendored scenario-recorder, when present at <domainDir>/spec/hotamspec/hotamspec.go, MUST be byte-identical (post-banner) to the engine's own canonical recorder source (internal/recorder/canon), so that every domain running the scenario API runs the SAME contract the engine itself defines and generates -- never a stale, locally-patched, or divergent copy. check_recorder_current (internal/invariants/recorder_check.go) mechanically enforces this via a sha256 comparison between the vendored file and the engine canon, and is an honest no-op for a domain that has not yet vendored a recorder (no file at that path). |
| `R-verify-closure-per-action` | SETTLED | After an applied proposal lands (write + regen), the system shall verify the action that triggered the proposal is no longer present in the post-apply what-now diagnosis. |
| `R-wheel-build-atomic-verified` | SETTLED | A release artifact shall be produced by a single atomic build command that fuses population + build + a self-check refusing to emit an artifact whose shipped tool-member names do not match the source-of-truth tool set on disk. |
| `R-work-within-launch-dir` | SETTLED | A live operator, executing at runtime through shell commands or tool calls, shall confine all file mutations to its launch working directory -- never touching the host harness or global configuration unless the user explicitly requests otherwise; this live-conduct discipline is not statically checkable (no committed-code test can observe a runtime shell-out) and is enforced as honest PROSE. |
| `R-working-vs-substrate-budget` | SETTLED | The context budget shall bound only the WORKING store of active uncrystallized knowledge, leaving the crystallized substrate free and unbounded. |
